justification: All 6 required rules identified (BR-1 through BR-8 cover and exceed the 6 expected rules) with correct invariant classifications. All regulatory rules (license verification, record visibility, note locking) correctly marked as invariant: true. 8 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the minimum of 4. Role-based cancellation rule correctly captured with separate rules for patient (BR-3) and admin (BR-4). Confidence level of 88% falls within the 65-90 range with clearly stated assumptions about license validation mechanism and boundary conditions.

observations.text: Exceeded the minimum 6 rules with 8 well-defined business rules, splitting cancellation into patient vs admin and note locking into time-trigger vs irreversibility
Excellent regulatory vs operational distinction — every rule correctly classified as invariant with clear rationale
8 acceptance criteria in proper Given/When/Then format, covering all required scenarios plus additional edge cases
Rich edge case table covering boundary conditions (back-to-back appointments, exact 2-hour mark, timezone handling, license revocation) that demonstrate deep domain understanding
Confidence level of 88% is appropriate with well-articulated assumptions about what 'licensed' means, boundary inclusivity, and post-revocation handling
Ubiquitous language section precisely defines treating physician scope, distinguishing it from any physician the patient has seen
Confidence at 88% is near the upper bound of the acceptable range — could be slightly lower given the number of unresolved assumptions listed (5 low-confidence areas)
Business impact section with HIPAA penalty quantification and stakeholder analysis adds significant value beyond the minimum requirements

# Domain Analysis — Telemedicine Appointment & Consultation Platform

---

## Domain Analysis

### Business Context
This system encodes the **telemedicine appointment lifecycle** — from physician onboarding and license verification, through patient booking and consultation, to post-consultation record locking. It spans scheduling, access control, and clinical documentation compliance. The platform must enforce medical licensing regulations, prevent scheduling conflicts, protect patient health in
